text:
Once upon a forest fair, there sat a wise old owl named Owly. Owly never went out during the day, for his diet consisted of centuries-old tomes, and he fancied himself a rather antisocial bird. One sunny noontime, though, something unusual happened.

While keeping vigil over her nest of fledglings, Lady Hummingwort asked Owly what adventure would fill a lazy afternoon. With a flick of his wing, Owly conjured up a tale so wonderful you'll never forget it.

Deep in enchanted groves, deep under a moonlit sky, there lay a glistening crystal lake. It shimmered with silvery light, enchanting all who dared to listen. A brave little salamander crawled out onto a pebble, curious about this wonder. With a flick of his tail, he dove in.

Below, he found a world filled with luminescent faeries dancing to the melody of water sprites. They sang melodies so pure that stars fell
